config.title: E-Scooters in Berlin — FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers on renting, where you can ride, parking, drinking, and the fines that catch tourists.

items.q: How do I rent an e-scooter in Berlin?
items.a: Download one of the operator apps (Bolt, Dott, Lime or Voi), add a payment card, then scan the QR code on any parked scooter to unlock it. You ride to your destination and end the trip in the app. Expect roughly 1 EUR to unlock plus about 0.20 to 0.28 EUR per minute, so a short central hop usually costs a few euros.

items.q: Where am I allowed to ride an e-scooter in Berlin?
items.a: On bike lanes and cycle tracks, and on the road where there is no bike lane. Riding on pavements and in pedestrian zones is forbidden and is the classic tourist fine. Many parks, including Tiergarten, are posted no-scooter zones, and the apps geofence a lot of green space so the scooter slows down or refuses to end the ride there.

items.q: Can I ride an e-scooter in Berlin after drinking?
items.a: Treat it exactly like driving a car. An e-scooter is legally a motor vehicle, so the 0.5 per mille blood-alcohol limit applies, and from 1.1 per mille it is a criminal offence. Penalties start around 500 EUR plus points and can include losing your driving licence. If you are under 21 or on a probationary licence, the limit is zero. After a beer garden, walk it or take the U-Bahn.

items.q: How much does a rental e-scooter cost in Berlin?
items.a: Pricing is per minute: usually around 1 EUR to unlock plus roughly 0.20 to 0.28 EUR per minute, depending on the operator and any day passes. That makes scooters cheap for a quick 10-minute hop but often more expensive than a normal BVG ticket for a longer cross-city trip.

items.q: Where can I park a rental e-scooter in Berlin?
items.a: Park parallel at the very edge of the pavement, leaving about 2.30 m clear for people and wheelchairs, and never in the middle of a walkway, a doorway or a tram track. Central districts increasingly require you to end the ride inside a designated bay, shown in the app with a blue outline or a Jelbi "J" symbol. Parking badly can cost 15 to 30 EUR.

items.q: Do I need a licence or a helmet to ride an e-scooter in Berlin?
items.a: You do not need a driving licence to ride a rental e-scooter, and helmets are not legally required (though I would wear one). The legal minimum age to ride is 14. In practice most rental apps require you to be 18 to unlock a scooter, and you always ride one person at a time.
